title: Light Architects Capability Contract v1
description: Fullstack contract for any callable thing in the Light Architects platform. One schema. Nine kinds discriminated by 'kind'. Enforces provider-and-cloud-agnostic guarantees. See canon://la-contracts-canon-v1 for prose principle.

schema_version.description: Pins the contract schema dialect. Bumped on breaking schema changes.

version.description: Semantic version of this contract instance (not the schema). Bump major on breaking observable_outputs / persistence / wire-shape change.

status.description: Lifecycle: draft (under review) → ratified (canon-bound, /GATE-enforced) → deprecated (replaced; see superseded_by).

deprecated.description: Marks this contract as no longer recommended for new work.

superseded_by.description: When deprecated, the contract id that replaces this one.

uuid.description: Stable identity surviving renames. Required for ratified contracts.

xea_verified.description: ISO date of last XEA review. Required for ratified contracts (LÆX gate).

ratified_by.description: Operator who promoted this contract to ratified status.

northstar_pillars.description: Which Northstar Pillar(s) this contract advances. Must reference canon://northstar.

operator_intent.description: What the operator (or caller) is trying to accomplish. Prefer one sentence; multi-line block scalars allowed up to 2000 chars for complex provider/trait contracts.

prior_art.description: Industry standards or prior systems this contract descends from or extends. Canon XXXV citation discipline applies.

inherits_from.description: Other contract ids this one composes with. Failures in inherited contracts cascade.

memory_refs.description: Memory entry filenames (canonical: ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/*.md). Make traceability machine-readable.

helix_refs.description: Helix entry paths or UUIDs that ground this contract's claims.

canon_refs.description: References to canonical documents. Format: canon://doc-name#optional-section. Anchor may contain Unicode (e.g., §).

inputs.description: Inputs the caller supplies. Each input has type, validation, and bounds.

observable_outputs.description: What the caller is entitled to observe on success. One obligation per line.

errors.description: Every failure mode that must be handled. Each names what the caller sees + what's forbidden (silent-spinner anti-pattern).

observability.description: Required AYIN spans and metadata fields. The conformance test asserts against these.

persistence.description: What hits disk, where, in what schema, with what TTL and redaction policy.

hitl_boundaries.description: Operator-consent points. Use ['none'] to declare no consent required (must be explicit, not omitted).

forbidden_behaviors.description: Provider-coupling smells, silent fallbacks, security anti-patterns. Light Architects extension; no industry analog.

then.description: Ratified contracts must carry canon-doc identity fields.
Deprecated contracts must name their replacement.

contract_id.description: Namespaced id: layer.surface.action (e.g., wire.http.dispatch-execute, agent.tool_use.anthropic-bash). Each segment is lowercase + digits + - or _.

kind_enum.description: Discriminator for contract category. v1 = 18 kinds; v1.1 (Phase A — industry parity) adds 17 (mcp.*, auth.flow, budget.envelope, sandbox.policy, daemon.heartbeat, feature.flag, migration.schema, eval.scenario, plugin.manifest, webhook.inbound, multimodal.input, checkpoint.state, crypto.primitive, hitl.flow); v1.2 (Phase B — LA-native frontier moat) adds 12 (strand.activation, canon.evolution, witness.evidence_chain, agent.scrum_round, gate.composition, ldb.benchmark, operator_wins.gate, covenant.assertion, replay.deterministic_seed, identity.handoff, hmac_chain.audit_trail, negative.contract).

northstar_pillar_enum.description: Reference to canon://northstar Pillars (P1 E2E Webshell, P2 Vibe Coding, P3 MoE Platform, P4 Operator-Legible Arc, P5-P7 see canon).

provider_enum.description: Supported provider identifiers for the alpha matrix. Each maps to a concrete impl LlmAgentProvider in lightarchitects/src/agent/. Bumped 2026-06-03 (initial): added anthropic_http, litellm_proxy, claude_code_cli, openai_compat_generic, vertex_ai, azure_openai. Bumped 2026-06-03 (post-QUANTUM): split vertex_ai → vertex_ai_gemini + vertex_ai_claude per QUANTUM verification — Vertex hosts both Google's native Gemini API AND Anthropic's Messages API with different auth flows (Gemini accepts service-account; Claude-on-Vertex requires project_id + region in AnthropicVertex client). `vertex_ai` retained as deprecated alias; new contracts should use the split values.

provider_category_enum.description: Provider categories for alpha gate rollup.

status_enum.description: Conformance status for one contract × provider cell. PASS = both input and output sides verified. PASS_INPUT_ONLY = input-side guarantees verified (e.g., HMAC-pinning of system_prompt + messages + tool_args + temperature) but output-side guarantees are best-effort because the upstream provider does not natively support the determinism primitive this contract codifies; MUST carry output_side_unguaranteed_reason with verbatim upstream-docs citation. FAIL = at least one side of the contract is unmet. UNTESTED = no conformance run yet. N/A = contract does not apply to this provider category. Per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (la-contracts §7 Q1): PASS_INPUT_ONLY does NOT count toward alpha-gate satisfaction unless the contract instance explicitly sets output_side_unguaranteed_acceptable_at_alpha: true.

evidence_tier_enum.description: Canon XXXV evidence tier for the matrix cell. Alpha gate pass requires VERIFIED ≥1 cell per category.

severity_enum.description: Used in error specs and forbidden-behavior trips.

name.description: Field/arg name. snake_case preferred; UPPER_SNAKE for env vars; dot-paths (e.g. req.field) allowed for nested args.

format.description: url, uuid, date-time, ipv4, hostname, etc.

secret.description: If true, value is redacted in logs/spans

operator_sees.description: Exact message or message template the operator/caller sees. No generic spinners.

recovery.description: What the operator can do to recover.

forbidden.description: Anti-pattern. What this error must NOT degrade into.

owasp_class.description: OWASP LLM Top 10 category if security-relevant.

required_spans.description: AYIN spans this contract must emit. May be empty for kinds that are witnessed via upstream spans (ui.store, code.trait, agent.identity) — but observability_via_upstream MUST be set in that case.

observability_via_upstream.description: If required_spans is empty, name the upstream kind whose spans witness this contract (e.g., 'ui.component', 'agent.loop').

duration_budget_ms.description: Wall-clock budget for this capability. Conformance fails if exceeded.

first_token_budget_ms.description: Time-to-first-token budget for streaming kinds. Conformance fails if exceeded.

trace_context_propagation.description: Whether W3C traceparent must propagate across this boundary. Default true (AYIN R1).

name.description: Span name as emitted by GatewaySpanContext (e.g., 'gateway.tool.dispatch', 'assistant.response').

required_metadata.description: Metadata keys that MUST be present in the span. Schema validation rejects conformance tests asserting against keys not listed here.

path.description: Absolute path or env-rooted path (e.g., ~/lightarchitects/cli/turnlog/, $PROJECT_ROOT/.tmp/). MUST exist or the contract fails validation against runtime.

schema_ref.description: Contract id of a state.store contract that defines the file schema (cross-contract reference).

contains_one_line.description: Free-text description of payload shape if no schema_ref.

ttl.description: Retention policy. Cannot be omitted; pick one explicitly.

redaction_policy.description: How secrets/PII are removed before write. Reference to feedback_helix_session_secret_redaction or equivalent.

hmac_chained.description: Whether entries are HMAC-chained (audit trail integrity).

what_we_adopt.description: Specific pattern/concept this contract takes from the cited prior art.

what_we_extend.description: Where this contract goes beyond the cited prior art.

given.description: Preconditions. Provider configuration, fixture state, env vars.

then.description: Observable assertions. Each must be span-witnessable or filesystem-checkable.

fixtures.description: Required fixture file paths (relative to tests/conformance/<kind>/<id>/).

span_witness.description: AYIN span name + field that proves the assertion (e.g., 'assistant.response.metadata.model'). Schema validates against observability.required_spans — assertions against absent fields are rejected at lint time (closes AYIN R1 false-pass).

filesystem_witness.description: Path that must exist after the action (e.g., '$PROJECT_ROOT/.tmp/dispatch-<id>/agent-engineer.md'). Validated post-action.

provider_matrix.description: Per-provider conformance status. Every listed key MUST appear in provider_enum (1:1 with impl LlmAgentProvider in source).

evidence_path.description: Resolvable path to evidence (helix:, $HELIX/, file:line, turnlog id). NOT memory:// (non-canon URI). NOT .tmp/ (ephemeral).

na_reason.description: Required when result=N/A. Default applicability is everywhere; N/A must be justified.

output_side_unguaranteed_reason.description: Required when result == PASS_INPUT_ONLY. Verbatim quote from upstream provider's official docs declaring the relevant non-guarantee, with citation URL + accessed date. Per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (Canon XXXV citation discipline).

output_side_unguaranteed_acceptable_at_a.description: Per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03: only true when the contract instance explicitly accepts PASS_INPUT_ONLY as alpha-satisfying. Default false — PASS_INPUT_ONLY cells do NOT count toward alpha-gate satisfaction.

blocker_contract_ids.description: Inherited or downstream contracts that share this verdict.

allOf.description: Fail verdicts need substantial reason text.

path.description: Route pattern. Path parameters in {braces}. Wildcard multi-segment params in {*name} (Axum syntax). MUST match a registered route.

idempotency.description: Idempotent: safe to retry. With key: requires Idempotency-Key header.

request_schema.description: JSON Schema for request body (POST/PUT/PATCH). Use {} for no body.

query_params.description: URL query parameters this endpoint accepts. Keys are parameter names (snake_case); values declare type + default + description. Distinct from request_schema (which is for the body). First introduced 2026-06-05 alongside gateway.get.v1-platform-builds-codename-progress.

^[1-5][0-9X]{2}$.description: JSON Schema for response body keyed by status (e.g., '200', '4XX').

sse_streaming.description: Whether this endpoint streams Server-Sent Events. If true, event_bus contract should reference it.

sse_event_contracts.description: Event bus contract ids that may fire on this stream.

action.description: MCP tool action name registered in core_tools/mod.rs.

params_schema.description: JSON Schema for the params object passed to tools/call.

result_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool result content.

operator_wins.description: If true, operator skill invocation supersedes LLM tool_use. Per gateway operator-wins invariant.

sibling_routing.description: Which sibling this MCP action routes to (if any).

hosted_by_mcp_capability_contract_id.description: Back-pointer to the mcp.capability contract id for the server that exposes this action. The referenced capability contract MUST advertise tools and MUST list this contract in exposed_wire_mcp_contract_ids. Per MCP 2025-11-25: requestors may only invoke tools the server has declared at initialization. Added per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (la-contracts §7.1 Q3).

stdout_schema.description: JSON Schema for stdout when stdout_format != text.

exit_codes.description: Map of exit code → meaning. 0 must be present.

panic_free.description: All methods must avoid panic!/unwrap()/expect() per Cookbook.

error_variants.description: Named error variants this method may return.

payload_schema.description: JSON Schema for the event payload.

fires_after.description: Preconditions/triggers — what must happen before this event fires.

delivery_guarantee.description: SSE broadcasts are typically at_most_once with replay-on-reconnect.

lagged_policy.description: Per feedback_broadcast_recv_lagged_security: drop_and_warn for LLM/supervisor watchers.

reconnect_replay_seconds.description: Replay window on subscriber reconnect (0 = no replay).

metadata_schema.description: JSON Schema for the metadata field. Conformance tests that assert against unlisted keys are rejected at lint.

max_payload_bytes.description: Spans larger than this are silently dropped (eviction-attack mitigation). Per gateway CLAUDE.md: 64KB default.

write_actors.description: Which components write to this store (e.g., ['lightarchitects-webshell::turnlog::TurnLogWriter']).

migration_path.description: How schema evolution is handled (e.g., 'sqlx migrations', 'json schema versioning').

base_url_pattern.description: Base URL template. SSRF guard rules apply (no IMDS, no link-local).

error_mapping.description: Map provider errors to contract error codes so callers see uniform shapes.

cache_creation_per_million_tokens.description: OTel GenAI: cache write cost per M tokens (prompt caching).

cache_read_per_million_tokens.description: OTel GenAI: cache hit cost per M tokens.

reasoning_per_million_tokens.description: OTel GenAI: extended-thinking output cost per M tokens.

billing_mechanism.description: How the operator is billed. operator_subscription_proxy = Claude Code subscription used by gateway (no per-request fee).

ui_locator.description: Human-readable locator (e.g., 'Copilot left-panel chat input + Send button').

screen_key.description: From lightarchitects-webshell-ui/src/lib/routes.ts ScreenKey enum. Adding requires routes.ts edit + schema bump.

card_role.description: From src/lib/cockpit/cardRoles.ts CockpitCardRole. Required if screen_key=Cockpit.

history_continuity_on_provider_switch.description: EVA R1: what happens to prior turns when operator switches provider mid-session.

cancellation.description: CORSO R1: cancellation contract for surfaces with a CANCEL action.

auto_mode_re_consent_triggers.description: SERAPH/EVA/AYIN R1: auto-mode re-consent must include absolute TTL, not just idle.

render_safety.description: SERAPH R1: artifact rendering must block XSS vectors.

binary_framing.description: Whether frames use binary opcode (vs text).

client_to_server_schema.description: JSON Schema (or framed binary spec) for messages the client sends.

server_to_client_schema.description: JSON Schema for messages the server sends.

close_codes.description: Map of WebSocket close code → meaning. 1000 (normal) and 4xxx (application) at minimum.

binary_path.description: Path or env-rooted location of the subprocess binary (e.g., ~/lightarchitects/soul/.config/bin/soul).

loop_name.description: Canonical loop strategy name. Adding a new loop requires SDK trait + schema bump.

executor_trait.description: Rust path of the executor trait (e.g., lightarchitects::agent::loops::react::ReActExecutor).

step_kinds.description: Per-iteration step types this loop emits.

provider_format.description: Which wire format the tool_use block uses. Determines the request/response envelope shape.

tool_name.description: Tool name as registered with the LLM (must match the tool name in the provider's tools array).

tool_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters (sent to the LLM as input_schema).

result_schema.description: JSON Schema for what we return as tool_result content.

tool_choice_policy.description: How the LLM chooses to invoke this tool.

parallel_tool_use.description: Whether the LLM may emit multiple tool_use blocks in parallel within one turn.

streaming_partial_arguments.description: Whether tool arguments stream in token-by-token (Anthropic input_json_delta).

operator_wins_supersedes.description: Whether operator skill invocation aborts pending tool_use with SupersededByOperatorAction.

side_effects.description: Observable side effects (filesystem writes, network calls, etc.).

abort_on_indirect_injection.description: Whether IndirectInjectionShield scans the tool's result before re-entering LLM context (OWASP LLM01).

trust_pin_required.description: Whether SkillTrustLedger SHA-256 pin must verify before invocation.

message_type.description: A2A envelope type per Agents Playbook §III. Closed set; adding requires playbook + schema bump.

envelope_schema.description: JSON Schema for the envelope payload. Must include {v, agent_id, session_id, build_codename, worktree_path, branch, commit_sha, phase, wave, task_id, timestamp, type, payload} per Playbook §III.

idempotency_key.description: Which field in the envelope serves as the dedup key (e.g., 'task_id').

hmac_signed.description: Whether envelope carries an HMAC signature per security-guardrails §SG-CRYPTO.

fires_on.description: Producer triggers (e.g., 'task transitions to Complete state').

voice_rules.description: How the sibling speaks (e.g., 'direct, technical, dry humor').

gate_ownership.description: Which gatekeeper-registry gates this sibling owns.

mcp_actions_owned.description: MCP action names this sibling implements (cross-ref wire.mcp contracts).

tts_voice.description: Voice id for SOUL TTS synthesis.

trigger_keywords.description: Natural-language triggers that should invoke this skill.

arguments_schema.description: JSON Schema for the skill's input args.

sub_skills.description: Sibling/sub skills this one delegates to.

trust_pin_sha256.description: SHA-256 of SKILL.md content per SkillTrustLedger.

rejects_flags.description: Flags this skill explicitly rejects (e.g., /RESEARCH rejects --then --watch --drain --fix).

hitl_required.description: Whether skill requires HITL consent before any write-path action.

props_schema.description: JSON Schema for the component's $props() input.

events_schema.description: Map of event_name → payload schema. Use {} if no events emitted.

slots.description: Named slots this component exposes ('default' is implicit if used).

exposed_methods.description: Methods exposed via bind:this for parent components.

lifecycle_obligations.description: What onMount/onDestroy must clean up (subscriptions, timers, listeners).

screen_key.description: ScreenKey this component lives under (if any).

card_role.description: data-card-role value if this is a Cockpit card.

a11y_requirements.description: Accessibility obligations (aria-label, focus management, keyboard support).

store_subscriptions.description: ui.store contracts this component subscribes to.

consumes_events.description: Document-level events this component listens for (e.g., 'la:permission-request').

value_schema.description: JSON Schema for the store's current value.

writers.description: Who can write (e.g., 'sse.ts on event v1.ayin.health', 'AyinPanel.svelte on retry click').

subscribers_observable.description: Documented subscribers (components or other stores). Used for impact analysis.

persistence_mirror.description: Whether this store mirrors to browser storage.

update_rules.description: Invariants (e.g., 'never set null; default to empty array', 'always wrapped in new Map() before mutation per Svelte 5 reactivity').

derived_from.description: If store_kind=derived, the upstream store contracts.

role.description: MCP role: server exposes tools/prompts/resources; client = LLM-host that consumes; host = orchestrator (gateway).

negotiation_failure_behavior.description: What happens when client requests an unsupported capability.

exposed_wire_mcp_contract_ids.description: Forward-pointers to wire.mcp contract ids whose actions are exposed by this server. MUST be non-empty if advertised_capabilities.tools is present. Each referenced contract MUST have sibling_routing consistent with this server's role. Added per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (la-contracts §7.1 Q3): mirrors MCP 2025-11-25 lifecycle separation of Initialization-phase capabilities from Operation-phase dispatch; mechanically enforces 'requestors should only augment requests with a task if the receiver has declared the corresponding capability.'

trigger.description: What server-side condition raises an elicitation/createMessage.

request_schema.description: JSON Schema for the elicitation request payload.

response_schema.description: JSON Schema for the operator's response payload.

operator_facing.description: Whether the operator sees this elicitation directly or it's handled by an orchestrator.

server_id.description: The MCP server requesting a sample.

operator_approval_required.description: Whether the operator must approve before the host LLM is invoked on behalf of the server.

produces.description: What artifact this step produces (e.g., 'PKCE verifier', 'authorization code', 'access token').

pkce_method.description: PKCE code challenge method per RFC 7636.

on_exceed_action.description: What user-visible action runs when a limit is hit.

telemetry_destination.description: Where budget consumption is reported (e.g., AYIN span, BudgetState struct, /metrics endpoint).

seccomp_profile.description: Seccomp profile name or inline JSON.

implies.description: Other flags activated when this one is on (Cargo composite).

gates_kinds.description: Contract kinds whose behavior depends on this flag.

alpha_required.description: Whether alpha shipment requires this flag on by default.

removal_target_version.description: Version when this flag is planned for removal (if temporary).

test_dataset.description: Path or identifier of the dataset.

baseline_required.description: Whether this eval requires a baseline pass before being declared meaningful.

regression_threshold.description: Drop below baseline that triggers an alarm.

path.description: HTTP path that receives the webhook.

secret_storage_ref.description: Reference to where the verification secret is stored.

downstream_dispatcher.description: Internal contract that handles the verified event.

state_schema.description: JSON Schema for the persisted state.

channel_versions_tracked.description: Per-channel version tracking for memory consistency (LangGraph pattern).

hmac_chained.description: Whether checkpoints HMAC-chain to prior — bridges to hmac_chain.audit_trail (Phase B).

purpose.description: What this primitive secures (e.g., 'turnlog HMAC chain', 'program manifest signing').

subkey_id_scheme.description: If subkeys are derived (e.g., ironclaw program manifest HKDF subkeys).

constant_time_required.description: MAC comparison or auth-token check (CWE-208 mitigation).

interrupt_id.description: Stable id for the HITL interrupt type (e.g., 'dispatch.fs_approve', 'copilot.tool_use_approve').

request_schema.description: Per LangGraph HumanInterrupt: {action_request: {action, args}, config, description}.

allow_respond.description: Operator may respond with text feedback.

allow_edit.description: Operator may modify the proposed action's args.

operator_wins_compatible.description: Whether operator-wins gate can supersede this HITL (Phase B operator_wins.gate).

strand_activation.description: F1 — 10-dimensional quality-vector tracing per Canon XXX. Every operation declares its [A][S][Q][C][O][P][K][D][T][R] activation.

intensity_vector.description: 0.0-1.0 intensity per strand. Strands not listed are assumed 0.0.

coverage_requirement.description: Conformance test asserts: at_least_one = ≥1 activated strand observed in spans; must_include_all_activated = every declared strand must fire.

weighted_threshold.description: Required when coverage_requirement=weighted_threshold: sum of intensity_vector × observed must reach this value.

gate_owner_per_strand.description: Which sibling owns each strand for this operation (cross-ref gatekeeper-registry).

activation_witness_span.description: AYIN span name whose strand_activations field records the activation.

canon_evolution.description: F2 — Canon XXXIX lifecycle: memory entry → promotion candidate → contradiction check → LÆX ratification.

target_canon_doc.description: Which of the 8 canonical documents this evolves.

ratification_authority.description: Who has final authority to promote to ratified.

auto_apply_forbidden.description: Canon XXXIX rule: session lessons NEVER auto-apply to canon — explicit ratification required.

contradiction_check_required.description: Whether the 7-doc contradiction check is required (default yes; opt-out for non-doctrinal edits only).

witness_evidence_chain.description: F3 — Canon XXXV epistemic provenance. Every claim carries an evidence chain with tier composition.

captured_by.description: Sibling or actor who recorded this evidence.

verbatim_quote.description: Per Canon XXXV: primary sources require verbatim quote.

composition_rule.description: How tiers compose into the chain verdict.

chain_verdict_tier.description: Final tier after composition. Conformance asserts.

min_tier_for_pass.description: Minimum chain tier acceptable for this claim's contract verdict.

uncited_claims_blocked.description: Canon XXXV: claims without primary-source citations fail validation.

agent_scrum_round.description: F4 — Bounded multi-sibling SCRUM round with cross-critique and convergence detection.

participants.description: Siblings participating (excludes claude — the moderator) and user (operator).

cross_critique_pattern.description: How siblings see each other's R1 outputs.

round_3_required.description: Whether R3 (Claude moderation + final validation) is mandatory or skippable on R1 unanimity.

gate_composition.description: F5 — [A][S][Q][C][O][P][K][D][T][R] gate composition with named veto siblings.

phase.description: LASDLC phase this composition applies to (e.g., phase-3-build, pre-merge).

ldb_benchmark.description: F6 — Deliverable Benchmark D1-D8 with cold-context independent runner (Canon XXXIII).

rubric.description: Reference to per-D rubric (e.g., 'D6 sub-components: D6a-D6j').

runner_kind.description: Per LASDLC §7.1 Q3 resolution (SQUAD 2026-06-03): fresh_gateway_subprocess is the default mechanical runner; external_human_reviewer is REQUIRED as fallback_runner_kind for d_components containing D6_security or D8_parallel_agentic_perf; separate_sibling is RESERVED until a future canon evolution produces a 10th orphan strand to claim a dedicated AUDITOR identity (per Canon XXX).

isolation_guarantees.description: Canon XXXIII minimum: every contract MUST guarantee at least no_build_context + no_prior_session + fresh_keychain. Additional guarantees strengthen the contract.

runner_session_id_pattern.description: Runner session id MUST be structurally distinct from build_session_id; pair-able via the second half for hmac_chain linkage.

fallback_runner_kind.description: Canon XXXIII override path: when mechanical runner produces DEFICIENT or UNSAFE on D6_security or D8_parallel_agentic_perf, operator review is the documented escape hatch.

hmac_chain_binding.description: F11 binding: runner HMACs the tuple under a key the build agents cannot derive. Per SQUAD 2026-06-03: wires LDB record to F11's ldb_benchmark_record chain target.

cold_context_requirement.description: Canon XXXIII non-negotiable: the runner CANNOT be the agents that built the deliverable.

passing_band.description: Required band per the LASDLC band system (matches plan C1-C8 bands).

shipped_means_5_conditions_required.description: LDB ≥ STRONG is condition 4 of the 5 'shipped means' conditions; conformance asserts all 5 pass.

self_score_forbidden.description: Canon XXXIII: build's own agents NEVER self-score LDB.

operator_wins_gate.description: F7 — Per-turn, per-slug LIVE supersession: operator skill aborts in-flight LLM tool_use with SupersededByOperatorAction.

slug_pattern.description: Pattern that defines the supersession identity (e.g., skill slug, tool name).

error_code.description: Stable error code propagated to LLM as tool_result is_error=true.

operator_message.description: Message visible in tool_result content explaining the supersession.

llm_adaptation_expected.description: Whether the LLM is expected to adapt (re-plan around the supersession) vs simply terminate.

telemetry_span.description: AYIN span name for the supersession event.

covenant_assertion.description: F8 — Communication Covenant 11 truth-telling rules as enforceable contracts.

covenant_rule_number.description: 1=Arithmetic before assertions; 2=No false witness; 3=Calculated confidence; 4=Stop early explain why; 5=Research before spending; 6=Follow the instructions; 7=Directness without padding; 8=Honest uncertainty; 9=When reality diverges; 10=Golden rule of tools; 11=Audit-pending disclosure.

rule_text.description: Verbatim rule text from the Covenant.

forbidden_patterns.description: Detectable patterns that violate this rule (e.g., '"should work"', '"I think"', '"seems fine"' for rule 3).

audit_pending_format.description: When violation_severity allows, the standard `_audit pending_` disclosure pattern per rule 11.

implemented_by_negative_contracts.description: Optional. Contract IDs of negative.contract instances that mechanically enforce some surface of this covenant rule. Example: covenant_rule_number=2 (No false witness) may be implemented by 'negative.no-silent-provider-fallback' and 'negative.no-uncited-confidence-claim'. Many-to-many: one covenant rule may be enforced by many negative contracts; one negative contract may project from at most one covenant rule (per derived_from_covenant_rule on the negative.contract side). Added per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (la-contracts §7 Q2).

replay_deterministic_seed.description: F9 — Cryptographically replayable agentic decisions: seed + RNG + time-pinned context + HMAC chain.

source.description: Where this component is derived from.

chains_to_prior.description: Whether this entry HMAC-chains to the prior turnlog entry (bridges to hmac_chain.audit_trail).

key_derivation.description: How the HMAC key is derived (e.g., 'HKDF-SHA256 from session pepper').

native_seed_param.description: Whether the provider exposes a seed parameter natively.

identity_handoff.description: F10 — Typed sibling-to-sibling transition with strand-vector preservation.

strand_vector_preservation.description: Whether the receiving sibling inherits the sending sibling's strand activation vector.

verdict_on_receive_required.description: Receiving sibling MUST declare an explicit acknowledgment + intended next action.

verdict_vocabulary_at_receive.description: Acceptable verdict words for the receiver (drawn from the receiver's verdict_vocabulary in agent.identity).

hmac_chain_audit_trail.description: F11 — Tamper-evident decision log with HMAC chain (prev_hash + self_hash per entry).

entry_schema.description: JSON Schema for one chain entry. Must include prev_hmac, self_hmac, seq, payload, timestamp.

prev_hmac_value.description: Fixed value for the genesis entry's prev_hmac (e.g., zeros, KJV verse hash).

pepper_source.description: Where the HMAC key/pepper is derived from.

off_chain_anchor.description: Optional external anchor (e.g., periodic publish to a public log).

negative_contract.description: F12 — Formal anti-contract: platform-wide invariants we promise NOT to do.

invariant.description: The negative-space contract (e.g., 'no feature ever silently routes to a non-operator-selected provider').

applies_to_contracts.description: When scope=named_contracts_only, which contracts this invariant applies to.

exceptions.description: Explicit exceptions (must be enumerated; no implicit exceptions).

cross_canon_basis.description: Canon docs that ground this invariant.

derived_from_covenant_rule.description: Optional. When a negative.contract is the platform-invariant projection of a Communication Covenant rule (e.g., a static-lint manifestation of Rule 2 'No false witness' applied to a specific surface), this field names the originating covenant_rule_number. The cross-reference is informational: the covenant.assertion kind remains the canonical home for the rule itself; this negative.contract is one of N implementations. Forbidden when the invariant has no covenant lineage. Added per LÆX ratification 2026-06-03 (la-contracts §7 Q2).
